intitule: Exercise 1: Understanding Reported Speech in Context (Resource)

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue between a student activist, Amina, and her teacher, Mr. Ndongo, about girls' education in their village. Then, answer the questions that follow to analyze how reported speech works.

Dialogue:

Amina: "I am organizing a community meeting to discuss school dropouts among girls."

Mr. Ndongo: "Your initiative is very brave, but you will need the support of the parents."

Amina: "Many families cannot afford school fees for their daughters."

Mr. Ndongo: "I know a local NGO that provides scholarships. They helped my niece last year."

Amina: "We must act now to change these old traditions."

questions.texte: Identify the exact words spoken by each character and then rewrite them as reported speech.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the direct speech: What did Amina say she is organizing?
sousQuestions.reponse: Amina said, "I am organizing a community meeting to discuss school dropouts among girls."

sousQuestions.texte: Transform the sentence from 1.a into reported speech. Start with: 'Amina announced that...'
sousQuestions.reponse: Amina announced that she was organizing a community meeting to discuss school dropouts among girls.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze the verb tense change: Which tense in direct speech ('am organizing') changed to which tense in reported speech ('was organizing')?
sousQuestions.reponse: The Present Continuous tense ('am organizing') changed to the Past Continuous tense ('was organizing').

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate Mr. Ndongo's statement about the NGO. Rewrite it in reported speech starting with: 'Mr. Ndongo mentioned that...'
sousQuestions.reponse: Mr. Ndongo mentioned that he knew a local NGO that provided scholarships and that it had helped his niece the previous year.

questions.texte: Apply the rules of reported speech to transform the following imperative and modal statements from the dialogue.

sousQuestions.texte: Transform Mr. Ndongo's advice: "You will need the support of the parents." (Use: 'Mr. Ndongo warned Amina that...')
sousQuestions.reponse: Mr. Ndongo warned Amina that she would need the support of the parents.

sousQuestions.texte: Transform Amina's statement of necessity: "We must act now." (Use: 'Amina insisted that...')
sousQuestions.reponse: Amina insisted that they had to act then / must act then. (Note: 'must' can remain 'must' or change to 'had to' for past obligation).

intitule: Exercise 2: Fighting Gender Discrimination in Your Community (Competence)

enonce.texte:
Real-Life Problem: You are the president of the 'School Club for Equality' in your Lycée in Yaoundé. You have just interviewed three people in your neighborhood about why many girls drop out of school after the first term of Terminale. You must present a report of their testimonies to the School Principal to request action. Use reported speech to relay their words accurately.

Testimonies (Direct Speech):

  • Mother (Mrs. Eto'o): "My daughter is very intelligent, but I cannot afford the extra fees for her math tutor. The boy's education is more important for our family's future."
  • Local Chief (Chief Manga): "We have a tradition here: girls must learn to run a household, not go to university. This is not a problem; it is our culture."
  • Teenage Girl (Fatima, dropped out): "My teachers ignored me when I asked for help. I will not go back because everyone expects me to fail."

questions.texte: Create a formal written report for the Principal. Write one paragraph for each testimony, accurately converting the direct speech into reported speech.
Justify your use of specific verb tenses and time expressions in your report. Explain two changes you made and why they are grammatically correct.
Propose one concrete action the school could take based on Fatima's testimony. Start your sentence with: 'Based on Fatima's claim that...' (Use reported speech to introduce your proposal).
questions.reponse: Based on Fatima's claim that her teachers had ignored her when she asked for help, the school could organize mandatory teacher training on supporting struggling female students.
